result:
## Summary

Diagnosed the site as a **mid-pivot SaaS**: it used to be *InboxZap* (email cleanup tool) and is now *Lumina* (AI meeting assistant), but only `app/page.tsx` had been updated for the new product. Everything else — metadata, nav links, blog, sitemap — was still stuck in the old product's shape, and the GSC data confirmed the damage (only the branded query "inboxzap" ranks; every legacy informational query sits at position 40–60 with near-zero clicks, and none of it matches what Lumina sells).

Fixes made:
- **`app/layout.tsx`** — replaced InboxZap title/description/OG/Twitter tags with Lumina's, added `metadataBase`, canonical, and a title template for child pages.
- **`app/page.tsx`** — added the missing `#faq` section (the `/faq` redirect pointed at a section that didn't exist) plus `SoftwareApplication` JSON-LD.
- **`app/features/page.tsx`, `app/pricing/page.tsx`** — created; nav and the homepage CTA linked here but neither page existed (404s).
- **Blog** — replaced the broken client-side `fetch('/api/posts')` pattern (no such API route existed; blog index always errored and was invisible to crawlers) with server-rendered static pages: `app/blog/posts.ts` (12 posts), `app/blog/page.tsx` (SSG index with real `<Link>`s), `app/blog/[slug]/page.tsx` (SSG per-post pages with `generateMetadata`). Kept the existing `post-1`…`post-12` slugs since GSC shows they're already indexed, but rewrote every post around Lumina/meeting-assistant keywords instead of the old inbox-zero/email-cleanup topics.
- **`app/sitemap.ts`** — removed the left-in TODO and now includes `/features`, `/pricing`, and all 12 blog posts.
- Caught and fixed a `'`-in-JS-string bug I introduced along the way (verified via a real production build + server, not just `next build`).

Verified with a full `next build` and a locally served production instance: correct titles/descriptions render, no leaked HTML entities, FAQ renders, all blog posts and new pages return 200, and the sitemap lists everything real. Note: the harness's own dev server on `127.0.0.1:60400` was unresponsive throughout this session (a separate process, not something I should touch), so I verified against a locally built instance instead.
